commit 9dc0a40883e2ab5824ef966de890e79c989e6326
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 23 19:50:34 2026 -0400

    fix: apply GraphQL bead perf harness review follow-ups [ddx-9ce6842a]
    
    Brings the review fixes from attempt 20260422T210754 into main. They
    were made on the attempt branch (6830cf4a) but never merged with the
    original land (bc9b5dac), so the cross-project Playwright smoke was
    still hitting the project-scoped route, the Makefile BENCH_REPORT_DIR
    was relative (broke when make was invoked from repo root), the perf
    fixture seeded the retired sessions.jsonl layout, and the recorded
    baseline predated the fixture alignment.
    
    - cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts: cross-project
      smoke now navigates /nodes/{nodeId}/beads and exercises the `beads`
      GraphQL query (AC §8). Mock matcher tightened to beadsByProject vs
      beads(.
    - cli/Makefile: BENCH_REPORT_DIR resolves to an absolute path so
      `make -C cli bench-graphql` from any cwd writes into the repo docs
      tree (AC §7 posture).
    - cli/internal/server/perf/fixtures.go: sessions fixture seeds the
      current pointer-only session-index shards instead of legacy
      sessions.jsonl so agentSessions bench reflects production shape.
    - docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.{md,json}: refreshed
      from the aligned fixture on this hardware (AC §2).

input.content:
No code changes are needed in this attempt.

(a) What is done
- The full bead deliverable already shipped to `main` in commit
  9dc0a408 ("fix: apply GraphQL bead perf harness review follow-ups
  [ddx-9ce6842a]") and its predecessors.
- HEAD of this worktree (7a2078a5, the pre-execute-bead checkpoint)
  has no diff against `main`; `git log main..HEAD` and
  `git log HEAD..main` are both empty.
- All acceptance criteria are already satisfied on disk:
  * AC §1 perf harness — `cli/internal/server/perf/{harness.go,
    fixtures.go,perf_test.go,smoke_test.go,report.go}` exist with
    the required query targets (bead, beadsByProject, beads
    cross-project, docGraph, agentSessions) and table-driven
    additions.
  * AC §2 baseline — `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md`
    and `.json` are present with the post-alignment p95 numbers
    quoted in the bead notes (2026-04-23 entries).
  * AC §3 BeadsByProject scope fix —
    `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:79` defines
    `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject`, and
    `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:40` calls it.
    `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:64) delegates to the
    project-scoped path when projectID is set.
  * AC §4 filter pushdown — status/label/projectID applied at the
    parse boundary (state_graphql.go scoped path).
  * AC §5 perf targets — captured in baseline; all met
    (beads_crossProject p95 ~63ms in-process, well under 200ms
    target).
  * AC §6 stale-read safety — covered by existing tests in the
    perf and graphql packages.
  * AC §7 `make bench-graphql` — `cli/Makefile:91` defines the
    target with absolute `BENCH_REPORT_DIR`.
  * AC §8 Playwright cross-project smoke — fixed in 9dc0a408 to
    navigate `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercise the `beads`
    query.
  * AC §9 cross-reference — present in the bead notes
    (2026-04-23 entry naming ddx-ad0db8fd and ddx-2ceb02fa).

(b) What is blocking
- Nothing on the implementation side. The REVIEW:BLOCK note in the
  bead is a review-payload visibility complaint ("the only commit
  shown is the execution-evidence chore"), not a code gap. Rerunning
  the review against the full branch diff that includes 9dc0a408
  and predecessors will resolve it without any new commits.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
- Not another implementation pass. The next step is either:
  1. Re-trigger the automated review with the correct diff payload
     (the full set of commits that introduce the harness, the
     scope fix, the filter pushdown, the Makefile target, the
     baseline files, and the Playwright spec change), or
  2. Manually close the bead now that the AC are demonstrably met
     on `main`.
- Committing here would only add a no-op chore on top of an
  already-merged deliverable and would not change the review
  outcome.

data.text: The bead's deliverable is already merged on `main` (commit `9dc0a408` and predecessors). HEAD equals main with a clean tree — no diff to commit. All AC items are satisfied on disk (perf harness, baseline, `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject` scope fix, filter pushdown, `make bench-graphql`, Playwright cross-project smoke, notes cross-reference). The REVIEW:BLOCK is a review-payload visibility issue, not a code gap. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` per execute-bead protocol.
